---
title: "Client Report - What’s in a name?"
subtitle: "Course DS 250"
author: "Travis Hineman"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
#| label: libraries
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from pandasgui import show
from datetime import datetime
```


## Elevator pitch

_paste your elevator pitch here_
_A SHORT (4-5 SENTENCES) PARAGRAPH THAT `DESCRIBES KEY INSIGHTS` TAKEN FROM METRICS IN THE PROJECT RESULTS THINK TOP OR MOST IMPORTANT RESULTS._

```{python}
#| label: project data
#| code-summary: Read and format project data
# Include and execute your code here
df = pd.read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4names/master/data-raw/names_year/names_year.csv")
#show(df)
```



## QUESTION|TASK 1

__How does your name at your birth year compare to its use historically?__

_Starting in 1910 till 1962, where children being named Travis began to increase. By the year 1978, 6,849 children had been named Travis. The transition between 1978 and 1979 would then experience a 32% increase in usage. For the years between 1979 and 1990, the name would see alot of fluctuation in how often it was used. 1990 would mark the peak usage as well as see the name's continual decline till the end of the data set. Specifically, between 1990 and 1998, my birthday, the name saw a 66% decline in usage. 2007 broke the continuous declines, with a small increase before continuing the decreasing decline._

```{python}
#| label: Q1
#| code-summary: Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
travis = df[df['name'] == 'Travis'] 
hl = travis[travis['year'] == 1998]
# peak = travis[travis['year'] == 1990]

# total_df1 = travis[travis['year'] == 1990]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df2 = travis[travis['year'] == 1998]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df3 = travis[travis['year'] == 1978]['Total'].values[0]
# total_df4 = travis[travis['year'] == 1979]['Total'].values[0]

# percent_decrease = ((total_df1 - total_df2) / total_df1) * 100
# print(percent_decrease)
# percent_decrease2 = ((total_df4 - total_df3) / total_df1) * 100
# print(percent_decrease2)
# show(travis)
```


```{python}
#| label: Travis Name Trends
#| code-summary: Code
#| fig-cap: "This graph shows the trends when it came to naming children Travis since 1910."
#| fig-align: center
# Include and execute your code here

fig = px.line(
    travis, 
    x = 'year', 
    y = 'Total',
    title = 'Travis Name Usage Trends since 1910',
    markers = True, 
    line_shape = 'spline'
) 
fig.add_scatter(
    x=hl['year'], 
    y=hl['Total'],
    mode='markers', 
    marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
    showlegend=False
)
fig.add_annotation(
    x=1999, 
    y=max(hl['Total'])+150, 
    text="My Birthday",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="darkgoldenrod",
    font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")
)
fig.add_vline(
    x=1990, 
    line_width=2, 
    line_dash="dash", 
    line_color="red"
)
fig.add_annotation(
    x=1991, 
    y=max(travis['Total'])+180, 
    text="Peak",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=1,
    ax=20,  
    ay=-30,
    arrowcolor="red",
    font= dict(color="red")
)
fig.update_layout(
    plot_bgcolor = 'white',
    xaxis_title = 'Year',
    yaxis_title = 'Total', 
    title_x = 0.5,
    title_font = dict(size = 24)
)
fig.update_xaxes( 
    showgrid = True, 
    gridcolor = 'lightgray'
) 
fig.update_yaxes(
    showgrid=True,  
    gridcolor='lightgray'  
)

fig.show()
```



## QUESTION|TASK 2

__If you talked to someone named Brittany on the phone, what is your guess of his or her age? What ages would you not guess?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
#| label: Q2
#| code-summary: Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
# brit = df[df['name'] == 'Brittany'] 
# c_yr = datetime.now().year

# brit['Age'] = c_yr - brit['year']
# #show(brit)
# peak2 = brit[brit['Age'] == 34]

```



```{python}
#| label: Q2 chart
#| code-summary: Code
#| fig-cap: ""
#| fig-align: center
# Include and execute your code here
# fig2 = px.line(
#     brit, 
#     x = 'Age', 
#     y = 'Total',
#     title = 'Most Likely Age of a Brittany',
#     markers = True, 
#     line_shape = 'spline'
# )
# fig2.add_scatter(
#     x=peak2['Age'], 
#     y=peak2['Total'],
#     mode='markers', 
#     marker=dict(color='darkgoldenrod', size=10),
#     showlegend=False
# )
# fig2.add_annotation(
#     x=peak2['Age'], 
#     y=peak2['Total'], 
#     text="Peak",
#     showarrow=True,
#     arrowhead=1,
#     ax=20,  
#     ay=-30,
#     arrowcolor="darkgoldenrod",
#     font= dict(color="darkgoldenrod")
# )
# fig2.add_vline(
#     x=brit['Age'].mean(), 
#     line_width=2, 
#     line_dash="dash", 
#     line_color="red"
# )
# fig2.add_annotation(
#     x=brit['Age'].mean()+1, 
#     y=10000, 
#     text="Mean",
#     showarrow=True,
#     arrowhead=1,
#     ax=20,  
#     ay=-30,
#     arrowcolor="red",
#     font= dict(color="red")
# )
# fig2.update_layout(
#     plot_bgcolor = 'white',
#     xaxis_title = 'Age',
#     yaxis_title = 'Total', 
#     title_x = 0.5,
#     title_font = dict(size = 24)
# )
# fig2.update_xaxes( 
#     showgrid = True, 
#     gridcolor = 'lightgray'
# ) 
# fig2.update_yaxes(
#     showgrid=True,  
#     gridcolor='lightgray'  
# )

# fig2.show()
```



## QUESTION|TASK 3

__Mary, Martha, Peter, and Paul are all Christian names. From 1920 - 2000, compare the name usage of each of the four names. What trends do you notice?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
#| label: Q3
#| code-summary: Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
# chris = df[df['name'] == 'Mary' |
#     df['name'] == 'Martha' |
#     df['name'] == 'Peter' |
#     df['name'] == 'Paul' 
# ] 
# chris = chris[chris['year'] > 1920]
# show(chris)

```


```{python}
#| label: Q3 chart
#| code-summary: plot example
#| fig-cap: "My useless chart"
#| fig-align: center
# Include and execute your code here

```


```{python}
#| label: Q3 table
#| code-summary: table example
#| tbl-cap: "Not much of a table"
#| tbl-cap-location: top
# Include and execute your code here


```


## QUESTION|TASK 4

__Think of a unique name from a famous movie. Plot the usage of that name and see how changes line up with the movie release. Does it look like the movie had an effect on usage?__

_type your results and analysis here_

```{python}
#| label: Q4
#| code-summary: Read and format data
# Include and execute your code here
bruce = df[df['name'] == 'Bruce']

```



```{python}
#| label: Q4 chart
#| code-summary: plot example
#| fig-cap: "My useless chart"
#| fig-align: center
# Include and execute your code here

```


```{python}
#| label: Q4 table
#| code-summary: table example
#| tbl-cap: "Not much of a table"
#| tbl-cap-location: top
# Include and execute your code here


```
